findings.one_line: The proof uses the observed-data score decomposition S = S_X + S_{A|X} + S_{Y|AX} and the conditional-score identities for \dot m_t, but that structure is not available from the declared depends_on set.

The claimed o_P(1) / o_P(n^{-1/2}) off-piece bounds rely on uniform bounds for estimated score and Taylor-remainder integrands after branch switches, but ass:band-regular-piece only controls derivatives on the true realized branches and does not bound the estimated derivatives on A_{L,k} or A_{U,k}.

The root-n CLT is routed through lem:score-consistency and lem:one-step-remainder, so with the off-piece-control gap unresolved the endpoint asymptotic normality claim is not fully proved under the stated assumptions.
